questions.question: Where was Elon Musk born?
questions.explanation: The material states Elon Musk was born on June 28, 1971, in Pretoria, South Africa's administrative capital.

questions.question: What was the name of the first software company Elon Musk co-founded in 1995?
questions.explanation: In 1995, Musk co-founded the software company Zip2.

questions.question: In what year did Elon Musk become an American citizen?
questions.explanation: The text states, 'Musk also became an American citizen in 2002.'

questions.question: When did Elon Musk first join the automaker Tesla?
questions.explanation: Musk joined the automaker Tesla as an early investor in 2004 and became its CEO and product architect in 2008.

questions.question: In what year was Twitter rebranded as X by Elon Musk?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk acquired Twitter in 2022 and rebranded it as X in 2023.

questions.question: What position did Elon Musk hold in the Trump administration after Trump's inauguration in early 2025?
options: Head of the Department of Innovation
Senior Advisor to the President and de facto head of DOGE
questions.explanation: After Trump was inaugurated as president in early 2025, Musk served as Senior Advisor to the President and as the de facto head of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).

questions.question: Which of the following book series did Elon Musk attribute his success to having read as an enthusiastic reader?
options: A Song of Ice and Fire
questions.explanation: Elon Musk attributed his success in part to having read The Lord of the Rings, the Foundation series, and The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy.

questions.question: At what age did Elon Musk sell his BASIC-based game Blastar?
questions.explanation: At age twelve, Elon sold his BASIC-based game Blastar to PC and Office Technology magazine for approximately $500.

questions.question: After his parents divorced in 1979, why did Elon, at around age 9, choose to live with his father?
options: His father offered him a car
His father had an Encyclopædia Britannica and a computer
His father promised to teach him how to fly a plane
questions.explanation: Elon chose to live with his father because Errol Musk had an Encyclopædia Britannica and a computer.

questions.question: What was Elon Musk's maternal grandfather, Joshua N. Haldeman, known for before his death?
options: Being a renowned chef and restaurateur
A Canadian chiropractor, aviator, and political activist in the technocracy movement
questions.explanation: His maternal grandfather, Joshua N. Haldeman, was an American-born Canadian chiropractor, aviator and political activist in the technocracy movement.
